tool.name: VCF filtering
tool.command:
mkdir -p vcf_filtered_tmp && mkdir -p vcf_filtered_directory && mkdir -p summary &&
bash '$__tool_directory__/VCF_filtering.sh' --input '${vcf_input}' --name '${vcf_input.element_identifier}' --order '${filter_order}' --max-missing-ind '${filter_ind_missing_data.MAX_MISSING_IND}' --max-missing-loci '${filter_loci_missing.MAX_MISSING_LOCI}' --min-gq '${filter_GQ.min_gq}' --min-dp '${filter_DP.MIN_DP}' --min-mac '${filter_mac.MAC}' --max-ho '${filter_het.MAX_Ho}'

param.name: vcf_input
param.label: Upload your VCF file
param.help: File containing DNA sequence data in VCF format. You can upload a simple file, multiple files or a dataset collection

param.name: filter_order
param.label: Select and order the filters to apply
param.help: Only the selected filters will be applied, in the exact order chosen here.

param.name: MAX_MISSING_IND
param.label: Maximum missing data per individual (MAX_MISSING_IND)
param.help: Maximum fraction of missing genotypes allowed per individual.

param.name: MAX_MISSING_LOCI
param.label: Maximum missing data per locus (MAX_MISSING_LOCI)
param.help: Maximum fraction of missing genotypes allowed per locus.

param.name: min_gq
param.label: GQ minimum
param.help: Genotypes with a GQ below this value will be set to missing.

param.name: MIN_DP
param.label: Minimum read depth (MIN_DP)
param.help: Minimum read depth (DP) per genotype required to retain it. Typical values depend on sequencing coverage: 1-5 for permissive filtering, 10-20 for standard filtering, and >=30 for stringent filtering.

param.name: MAC
param.label: Minimum minor allele count per SNP (MIN_MAC)
param.help: Minimum number of minor alleles required for a locus to be retained.

param.name: MAX_Ho
param.label: Maximum observed heterozygosity per SNP
param.help: Maximum proportion of observed heterozygotes allowed to retain a variant. Used to detect paralogues and genotyping errors.

data.name: summary
data.label: Filtering : Number of SNPs and individuals

citations.citation: @article{10.1093/gigascience/giab008, author = {Danecek, Petr and Bonfield, James K and Liddle, Jennifer and Marshall, John and Ohan, Valeriu and Pollard, Martin O and Whitwham, Andrew and Keane, Thomas and McCarthy, Shane A and Davies, Robert M and Li, Heng}, title = "{Twelve years of SAMtools and BCFtools}", journal = {GigaScience}, volume = {10}, number = {2}, year = {2021}, abstract = "{SAMtools and BCFtools are widely used programs for processing and analysing high-throughput sequencing data.}", issn = {2047-217X}, doi = {10.1093/gigascience/giab008}, url = {https://doi.org/10.1093/gigascience/giab008}, note = {giab008}, }

@article{hemstrom2024next, author={Hemstrom, William and Grummer, Jared A. and Luikart, Gordon and Beja-Pereira, Albano and Waples, Robin S. and Funk, W. Chris and Shafer, Aaron B. A. and Allendorf, Frederick W.}, title={Next-generation data filtering in the genomics era}, journal={Nature Reviews Genetics}, volume={25}, number={11}, pages={750--767}, year={2024}, doi={10.1038/s41576-024-00738-6}, url={https://doi.org/10.1038/s41576-024-00738-6} }

@article{Danecek2011, author = {Danecek, Petr and Auton, Adam and Abecasis, Goncalo and Albers, Cornelis A. and Banks, Erica and DePristo, Mark A. and Handsaker, Robert E. and Lunter, Gerton and Marth, Gabor T. and Sherry, Stephen T. and McVean, Gil and Durbin, Richard and {1000 Genomes Project Analysis Group}}, title = {The variant call format and {VCF}tools}, journal = {Bioinformatics}, volume = {27}, number = {15}, pages = {2156-2158}, year = {2011}, doi = {10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, URL = {https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, }
